title: ReflectionTemplate
description: One voice-tested reflection template. The library (T-ML-040, docs/04 §'The prose reflection') is the human-curated input to the M2 reflection pipeline (T-ML-042): the engine selects one or more templates whose `applies_when` block matches a trait vector, passes the chosen template (including voice notes + exemplars) to the LLM, then runs the output through the safety + tone classifiers. Changes to anything under /content/reflection-templates/ require human review per AGENTS.md §10.6.

id.description: Stable identifier; must equal the basename of the file. Used by the audit log to identify which template fed each generated reflection.

version.description: Bump when prose or selection criteria changes in a way that would alter outputs for existing trait vectors. The replay tool compares this to the stored value.

summary.description: One-line description of the profile this template addresses. Surfaced in the review queue UI.

applies_when.description: Selection criteria. Template is a candidate when the trait vector satisfies ANY of the signal groups (logical OR). Within a signal group, ALL predicates must hold (logical AND).

priority.description: Higher = stronger candidate when multiple templates match. Single-dimension templates run at 50; contrast / multi-dimension templates run 60-80; meta + fallback templates run <30.

emphasize.description: Voice rules to weight up in the LLM prompt for this profile. Drawn from docs/04 §'Voice rules'.

avoid.description: Phrases or shorthands the LLM must not produce for this profile. Used by both the prompt assembler and the tone classifier.

notes.description: 1-3 sentences of free-form voice direction. Surfaced to the LLM in the prompt and to human reviewers in the queue.

exemplars.description: Hand-written sample outputs in the canonical voice. Used as few-shot examples in the LLM prompt and as regression anchors in tests.

signal_moments.description: 1-3 concrete in-game moments the exemplar grounds itself in. Each is a short phrase ("the song you played alone").

output.description: The reflection text itself, 3-5 sentences in second person.

notes.description: What this exemplar is meant to demonstrate (e.g. 'reframes the curious-impulsive read as a single underlying decision style').

voice.description: Only one voice is allowed today. Kept as an enum so future Echo personas (e.g. a B2B clinician variant) can extend safely.

forbidden_terms.description: Terms the tone + safety classifiers must reject. Defaults defined in services/ml-py apply to every template; this list adds template-specific extras.

signal.description: A conjunction of dimension predicates. Use a single-element all_of for one-dimension templates.

direction.description: How the predicate reads the dimension. For bipolar (Big Five, Schwartz) values in [-1, 1]: high = value >= min_magnitude; low = value <= -min_magnitude; prominent = |value| >= min_magnitude; muted = |value| < min_magnitude. Attachment values in [0, 1] only support high/prominent (>= min_magnitude) and low/muted (< min_magnitude).

min_magnitude.description: Threshold for the predicate to fire. Conventional values: 0.3 (mild signal), 0.5 (clear signal), 0.7 (strong signal).
